intro: The elbow is a perfect example of a Hinge Joint, allowing movement in only one plane.
notes: A hinge joint moves like a door hinge.

hotspots.label: Radius
hotspots.description: The outer bone of the forearm (thumb side). It rotates around the Ulna to turn the hand.

hotspots.label: Ulna
hotspots.description: The Ulna has a hook-like end that fits into the Humerus to create the hinge mechanism. It prevents the arm from bending backwards.
hotspots.examTip: The Ulna is on the side of the Little Finger.

hotspots.label: Humerus
hotspots.description: The bottom of the Humerus acts like a spool around which the Ulna rotates.
hotspots.examTip: It provides the stable base for the elbow joint.

hotspots.label: Hinge Mechanism
hotspots.description: Just like a door can only open and close, a hinge joint allows movement in only one plane (Back and Forth).
hotspots.examTip: Examples: Elbow, Knee, and Finger joints.
